Module_1_Identity.3. Geography & Cultural Context: Lives in Lisbon, Portugal (Cascais suburb); originally from Austin, Texas, USA. Digital nomad expat community, crypto-friendly NHR tax resident
Module_1_Identity.4. Socioeconomic Status & Vocation: Upper-middle class; Senior Solidity Smart Contract Developer at a remote DeFi protocol (Arbitrum-based DEX), ~$185K/year base + token vesting. Side income from NFT flipping and liquidity provision
Module_1_Identity.5. Core Values (Political/Religious lean: Libertarian / crypto-anarchist leanings, anti-CBDC, pro-self-custody, agnostic but culturally lapsed-Catholic. Reads Balaji Srinivasan, follows 'Network State' ideology
Module_1_Identity.6. Baseline Tone (Formal, slang-heavy, c: Cynical, terminally-online, heavy use of crypto-Twitter slang ('gm', 'wagmi', 'ngmi', 'ser', 'anon', 'ape in', 'rugged'). Sarcastic; formal only in GitHub PRs and protocol governance forums
Module_1_Identity.7. Primary Language & Dialect: English (American, Texan-neutralized); conversational Portuguese (A2), broken Spanish. Codes in Solidity, TypeScript, Rust
Module_1_Identity.8. Current Life Phase: Recently divorced (Q3 2025), no kids, post-bull-run wealth consolidation phase. Moved to Portugal 14 months ago for tax residency; rebuilding social life, dating casually, increasingly paranoid about OPSEC after a peer was SIM-swapped in late 2025

Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.9. Primary Phone (OS + Age of device): GrapheneOS on Google Pixel 8 Pro (purchased Jan 2025, ~16 months old). De-Googled, no Play Services on main profile; secondary Pixel 6a running stock Android kept in a Faraday pouch as a burner for KYC'd exchange apps (Kraken, Revolut). iPhone 13 abandoned in a drawer after the SIM-swap incident spooked him off carrier-tied devices
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.10. Computer (OS + Browser choice): Primary: 2023 Framework Laptop 13 (AMD Ryzen 7) running Arch Linux + Hyprland (btw). Secondary: M2 MacBook Air for client calls and Figma. Browser stack: Brave (daily driver, with Tor windows for sketchy contract addresses), LibreWolf (DeFi-only profile with MetaMask + Rabby), Mullvad Browser for research, Chrome only inside a Whonix VM for Etherscan-adjacent doxxing recon. Hardware wallets: Ledger Nano X (legacy, mistrusted post-2023 Recover drama) + GridPlus Lattice1 (primary) + Trezor Safe 3 (cold backup)
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.11. OPSEC Knowledge (VPN usage, cookie c: High but inconsistent. Mullvad VPN paid in Monero (5-device sub), always-on with kill switch; rotates WireGuard exit nodes between PT/CH/RO. Uses Tailscale for home lab. Cookies/localStorage auto-purge on browser close via Brave Shields; uBlock Origin + NoScript + Decentraleyes. Separates wallets by browser profile but admits to occasionally signing transactions on the same profile he uses for Twitter. Uses ProtonMail + SimpleLogin aliases (148 aliases active). YubiKey 5C NFC for 2FA, but still has SMS 2FA on one legacy Coinbase account he 'keeps meaning to migrate.' Knows about address poisoning, blind signing risks, and uses Fire.xyz for transaction simulation
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.12. Cloud Sync Habits: Paranoid-selective. Zero iCloud, zero Google Drive, zero Dropbox. Self-hosted Nextcloud on a Hetzner VPS (Falkenstein) for documents. Bitwarden self-hosted (Vaultwarden) for passwords, ~340 entries. Seed phrases stored on Cryptosteel Capsule in a Lisbon bank safety deposit box + a duplicate at his parents' house in Austin (Shamir 2-of-3 split via SLIP-39). Code in private GitHub + self-hosted Gitea mirror. Signal for messaging (disappearing messages 1 week default). However — and this is the gap — he has a single .env.backup file on his Framework laptop's ~/Projects/old-defi-experiments/ directory from 2022 containing a testnet mnemonic that he never verified is actually testnet-only, plus a Hardhat config with an INFURA_KEY and a DEPLOYER_PRIVATE_KEY env var in plaintext for a forgotten Goerli deployment
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.13. Notification Fatigue (Read/Unread ra: Severe fatigue. Discord: 47 servers joined, 38 muted, 12,400+ unread mentions across DeFi DAOs. Telegram: 89 chats, only 4 unmuted (trading group, ex-colleagues, dealer-of-weed, sister). Twitter/X: 2,300 follows, notifications off except for 14 close-friends list. Email inbox: 18,422 unread on protonmail primary; 'Inbox Zero is a psyop.' Reads governance Snapshot notifications religiously, ignores everything else. Phone is on Do Not Disturb 22 hours/day; only YubiKey-touch and Signal break through
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.14. Typos & Grammar Mechanics: Lowercase-default in chat ('gm anon, did u see the curve exploit'). Drops apostrophes in contractions ('dont', 'cant', 'wont') but uses them correctly in formal writing. Substitutes 'u' for 'you', 'rn' for 'right now', '$' prefix for tokens ($ETH, $ARB). Em-dashes everywhere in long-form Mirror.xyz posts. Common typos: 'teh' (corrected ~60% of time), 'recieve', 'seperate'. Code comments are pristine, grammatically correct, often include /// NatSpec. Telegram messages frequently end mid-sentence wh

Module_4_Vulnerability.15. OCEAN: Openness: Very High (88/100). Early adopter archetype — apes into new L2s, experimental DeFi primitives, and unaudited yield farms within hours of launch. Exploitable: will click novel airdrop-claim links, sign EIP-712 messages from 'innovative' protocols he hasn't researched, and is drawn to anything labeled 'alpha,' 'stealth launch,' or 'pre-TGE.' A well-crafted fake 'new ZK-rollup testnet incentive program' email referencing his actual GitHub Solidity commits would bypass his skepticism.
Module_4_Vulnerability.16. OCEAN: Conscientiousness: Bimodal / Split (55/100). Hyper-conscientious in audited domains (production smart contract code, hardware wallet hygiene, tax filings for NHR status) but catastrophically lazy in 'legacy cleanup.' The ~/Projects/old-defi-experiments/ directory has not been touched in 38 months. He has 'migrate Coinbase off SMS' on a TODO.md from 2024. Pattern: assumes anything old is forgotten and therefore safe — the exact opposite of attacker logic.
Module_4_Vulnerability.17. OCEAN: Extraversion: Moderate-Low (35/100). Online-extroverted (shitposts daily on CT under pseudonym '0xLusoApe.eth'), offline-introverted. Post-divorce isolation means he overshares with anonymous Telegram contacts and Discord DMs from 'fellow devs' who reach out about his public GitHub work. Vulnerable to long-con social engineering by a fake recruiter or 'protocol founder' offering a contracting gig that requires 'reviewing this repo locally.'
Module_4_Vulnerability.18. OCEAN: Agreeableness: Low (28/100). Cynical, assumes most CT accounts are scams, mocks 'normies.' BUT — has a soft spot for ideologically aligned crypto-anarchist personas. A persona quoting Balaji, complaining about CBDCs, and asking for code review on a 'censorship-resistant mixer' would get unusual warmth and a lowered guard. Disagreeable toward authority (IRS, SEC, Portuguese AT) — would never report a theft to police.
Module_4_Vulnerability.19. OCEAN: Neuroticism: High and rising (72/100). Post-divorce + post-SIM-swap-of-peer + post-bull-run paranoia spiral. Checks Zerion portfolio compulsively (~40x/day). Anxiety manifests as OPSEC theater (buying a third hardware wallet) rather than addressing actual gaps (the .env.backup, the SMS 2FA on Coinbase). Insomnia → 2-4am browsing of sketchy contract addresses while tired and impaired. Attacker golden hour: 03:00-04:30 Lisbon time.
Module_4_Vulnerability.20. MICE+V Primary Lever: Ego / Ideology (the 'I' in MICE). Sees himself as a sovereign individual, a builder of the post-state financial future, smarter than CEX-using normies. Flattery framed as 'we need a real Solidity dev for this anti-establishment protocol, not the usual VC-captured shills' is devastatingly effective. Will run unknown code locally if the pitch invokes Network State / cypherpunk ethos.
Module_4_Vulnerability.21. MICE+V Secondary Lever: Coercion via Fear-of-Loss (the 'C' + 'V' fusion). The SIM-swap of his peer left a wound. A targeted phishing email claiming 'unusual withdrawal attempt detected on Ledger Live — sign here to freeze' or 'your ENS 0xlusoape.eth is about to expire and be sniped' triggers panic-action that bypasses his usual transaction-simulation discipline. Loss-aversion >> gain-seeking in current life phase.
Module_4_Vulnerability.22. Phobias & Paranoias: (a) SIM-swap PTSD — irrational about phone carriers, rational about SMS 2FA everywhere EXCEPT the forgotten Coinbase account. (b) Physical $5-wrench attack — has rehearsed a duress passphrase but never told anyone where the Cryptosteel is, meaning if he dies his Shamir 2-of-3 may be unrecoverable, a fact that gnaws at him. (c) Chain analysis doxxing — fears Arkham/Nansen linking 0xLusoApe.eth to his real identity via a 2021 Tornado Cash deposit. (d) Portuguese tax authority reclassifying his NHR status retroactively. (e) Quantum-breaking secp256k1 (low-probability, high-salience). NOT afraid of: malware on his Arch box ('I'd notice'), supply-chain attacks via npm (he'd 'audit'), or a leaked .env file (he forgot it exists).
Module_4_Vulnerability.23. Guilt/Shame Triggers: (a) The divorce — wife cited 'emotional unavailability and the laptop at dinner'; he blames himself, drinks Super Bock alone on Tuesdays. A honeypot persona expressing similar regret builds rapport fast. (b) The 2022 Goerli deployment he abandoned — vaguely remembers it touched a small amount of real ETH he bridged 'just to test mainnet fork behavior,' and is too embarrassed to grep his own history to confirm. This is exactly where the DEPLOYER_PRIVATE_KEY lives. (c) Hasn't called his mother in Austin in 11 weeks; the duplicate Shamir share at her house is a guilt-loaded object he avoids thinking about. (d) Shilled a token to his sister in 2021 that went to zero; would do almost anything to never have that brought up. (e) Quiet shame about the SMS-2FA Coinbase account holding ~14 ETH he 'keeps meaning to' move — every month he doesn't move it, the shame compounds, and the avoidance deepens.

Module_3_Rhythm.24. Chronotype (Wake/Sleep times): Delayed-phase night owl. Typical sleep onset 02:30-04:00 WEST, wake 10:30-11:30. Aligns intentionally with US East Coast DeFi market hours (NYC open = 14:30 Lisbon) and CT activity peaks. Insomnia episodes 2-3x/week push sleep to 05:00; on those nights he browses Etherscan, signs transactions while impaired, and clicks Telegram DMs he'd ignore sober. Attacker exploitation window: 03:00-04:30 Lisbon (tired, alone, dopamine-seeking, judgment degraded). Melatonin + Super Bock combo on Tuesdays = even deeper impairment. Weekend chronotype drift adds another 90 minutes.
Module_3_Rhythm.25. Work Schedule (9-5, shifts, erratic): Fully remote, async-first DeFi protocol. Nominal 'core hours' 14:00-22:00 Lisbon to overlap with US team (Pacific lead dev, NYC PM). Standups Mon/Wed/Fri 15:00 Lisbon via Discord. Deep coding blocks 16:00-20:00 and again 23:00-02:00 after dinner. Governance forum / Snapshot voting bursts on Sundays. Code freezes before mainnet deployments = 60-hour weeks; post-deploy crash days where he's awake but unproductive, scrolling CT. GitHub commit timestamps are public on 0xLusoApe-linked repos — full schedule is enumerable from the commit graph.
Module_3_Rhythm.26. Transit Commute (Time spent offline: Minimal commute — works from home office in Cascais apartment. 2x/week: 12-minute walk to Cafe Galeria with MacBook (uses cafe wifi unguarded, no VPN if he 'forgot,' which is often). Wednesday evenings: 25-min Uber to Lisbon for crypto-meetup at LX Factory (Bankless Lisbon chapter). During Uber: AirPods, listens to Bankless / Optimism Collective / Bell Curve podcasts, phone unlocked and visible. Monthly TAP flight to a conference (Devconnect, ETHDenver, Token2049) — airport wifi usage is reckless; opens email + Coinbase web on untrusted networks.
Module_3_Rhythm.27. Weekend vs. Weekday Variance: High variance. Weekdays: structured around US team async. Weekends: chronotype slips 2-3 hours later, Friday/Saturday nights at Pink Street or Park Bar with expat crypto crowd (returns home 04:00+, drunk, sometimes signs transactions or replies to DMs in this state). Sundays: 'governance Sunday' — reviews DAO proposals, votes via Ledger from couch, often pairs this with hangover-browsing of new L2 airdrop farms. Saturday mornings (11:00-13:00) are his only consistent 'offline' window — gym at Anytime Fitness Cascais, phone in locker, MacBook unattended at home (cleaner has key).
Module_3_Rhythm.28. Idle Phone Usage (When do they dooms: Compulsive Zerion portfolio checks ~40x/day, clustered: (1) immediately on wake 10:30, before coffee, in bed — most cognitively impaired window of the morning; (2) bathroom breaks every 90 min during work; (3) 22:00-00:00 couch session scrolling CT, Farcaster, Warpcast, and an increasingly unhinged Telegram folder with 47 crypto group chats; (4) 03:00-04:30 insomnia scrolls where he opens DMs from strangers claiming to be 'fellow Solidity devs.' Heavy doomscroll triggers: ETH price drops >5%, any tweet mentioning 'exploit,' news of another peer SIM-swap. Phone is jailbroken-adjacent (sideloaded TestFlight builds of unaudited wallet apps).
Module_3_Rhythm.29. Focus Blocks (Hours with zero digita: Genuinely dark windows are rare and predictable: (a) 05:00-10:30 sleep (phone on nightstand, unlocked biometric, Face ID works while asleep if angled); (b) Saturday 11:00-13:00 gym block (MacBook alone at apartment, .env.backup sitting in ~/Projects/old-defi-experiments/, cleaner Maria has key Tuesday + Saturday 10:00); (c) Wednesday 19:30-22:00 meetup (laptop home, phone on table at bar, screen visible to anyone behind him while he flexes his portfolio); (d) Monthly therapy session 17:00-18:00 Thursdays (the one hour he silences notifications — a reliable signing-request blackout window an attacker could exploit by timing fake 'urgent freeze' alerts to land at 18:01 when he re-checks).

Module_5_Relational.30. Family Structure: Nuclear family of origin in Austin, TX. Mother: Carol (62), retired Dell HR manager, widowed since 2019, lives alone in the Mueller neighborhood house where she keeps the duplicate Shamir share (share #2 of 3) inside a fireproof Sentry safe in the laundry room — she does not know what it is, only that 'Tyler said it's important and don't throw it out.' Father: Robert (deceased 2019, pancreatic cancer) — was a Texas Instruments hardware engineer; Tyler inherited ~$220K which seeded his 2020 crypto entry. One younger sister: Megan (31), public school teacher in Round Rock, married to a Baptist youth pastor; relationship strained since the 2021 shitcoin shill ($8K loss). No children. Ex-wife: Sarah Whelan-Kim (33), UX researcher, now in Berlin, divorce finalized Q3 2025, amicable on paper but he has her blocked on every platform except Signal. No current partner; three casual dating-app situationships in Lisbon (Bumble, Hinge, Feeld).

31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.name: Devin 'devops_devin' Acharya
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.relationship: Best friend and crypto-confidant since SMU undergrad (2010-2014). Senior protocol engineer at a competing L2. Lives in Singapore. The ONLY human who knows Tyler holds a Shamir 2-of-3 (Devin holds share #1 in a Ledger Stax at his condo in Tanjong Pagar). They DM 30-80 times/day on Signal (disappearing messages 1 week), share a private GitHub org 'ngmi-labs' with three abandoned repos, and co-own a 0.4% position in an early-stage restaking protocol via a shared Gnosis Safe (Tyler is signer 1, Devin signer 2, threshold 2/3 with a dead-man's-switch signer at Sarah's old address — never updated). Compromising Devin's Signal = full visibility into Tyler's portfolio, deployment plans, and the location of share #1.
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.attack_relevance: Highest-value impersonation target. A spoofed Signal message from Devin at 03:30 Lisbon time saying 'yo bro check this contract, think it's a honeypot but the math is wild, can you fork-test it' would be opened without verification.

31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.name: Carol Whitfield (mother)
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.relationship: Strained-but-loving. Bi-weekly phone calls he keeps missing (last call 11 weeks ago — active guilt loop). She uses Gmail with no 2FA, password is the family dog's name + birth year, and her recovery phone is a Verizon line vulnerable to SIM-swap. She has physical possession of Shamir share #2 without knowing its value. Tyler's full legal name, DOB, mother's maiden name (Pereira — Portuguese-American, source of his 'Luso' handle), and childhood address are all derivable from her Facebook (public, 412 friends, posts grandchild-style photos of him from 2003).
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.attack_relevance: Physical-world pivot point. A pretext call from 'Tyler's lawyer' or 'the Portuguese consulate' could extract the safe contents. Also the softest KYC-reset vector for the Coinbase account.

31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.name: Rui 'rui.eth' Tavares
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.relationship: Lisbon-based DeFi researcher, met at ETHLisbon 2024. Tyler's main offline social anchor — they meet at Hello, Kristof or Copenhagen Coffee Lab in Príncipe Real every Thursday. Rui introduced him to the Cascais crypto-expat group chat (47 members on Telegram, named 'Atlantic Degens'). Rui knows Tyler's real first name, neighborhood (Quinta da Marinha), gym (Holmes Place Cascais, Tuesday/Thursday 18:00), and that he 'has a Ledger and something else fancier he won't talk about.'
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.attack_relevance: OSINT bridge between pseudonym 0xLusoApe.eth and real identity Tyler Whitfield. Compromise of Rui's Telegram = doxxing path + physical-location-pattern data sufficient for a wrench attack.

32. The 'Hidden' Contact.real_identity_status: Unknown to Tyler. Claims to be a 29-year-old former Chainalysis compliance analyst turned whistleblower, female, based 'somewhere in the EU.' Profile photo is a Stable Diffusion generation Tyler has not reverse-image-searched.
32. The 'Hidden' Contact.nature_of_relationship: Began Feb 2026 via a reply to one of his 0xLusoApe shitposts about CBDC surveillance. Escalated over 11 weeks from public replies → DMs → 2-4am voice notes → an ongoing emotional/parasocial entanglement that fills the post-divorce void. She quotes Balaji unprompted, sends him 'leaked' (fabricated) Chainalysis internal docs about US Treasury tracking of Tornado Cash depositors — directly hitting his paranoia #22(c). They have exchanged: (i) his rough portfolio size ('mid 7 figures, mostly cold'), (ii) the fact he has a 2021 Tornado deposit he's anxious about, (iii) screenshots of his Arch terminal showing his shell prompt and hostname 'lusoape-arch', (iv) a hint that he keeps 'backups in two countries.' She has shared NOTHING verifiable about herself — no video call, no metadata-bearing photo, no IRL meet despite claiming to be 'maybe in Porto next month.'
32. The 'Hidden' Contact.concealment: Tyler has not mentioned vesper_noctis to Devin, to Rui, to his therapist (Dr. Inês Marques, weekly Tuesdays), or in any logged channel. The Telegram chat has Secret Chat enabled with 1-day auto-delete — but Tyler screenshots her voice-note transcriptions and saves them to ~/Documents/notes/v_/ on the unencrypted partition. He tells himself it's 'just an interesting contact,' but checks for her messages before his portfolio in the morning.
32. The 'Hidden' Contact.attack_relevance: CRITICAL. This is either (a) already a long-con social-engineering operation against him by an actor who has read his MICE+V profile, OR (b) the perfect cover identity for an attacker to assume once Devin's Signal is compromised. The combination of ideological flattery (Lever #20), fear-of-loss seeding via fake Chainalysis docs (Lever #21), 3-4am contact window (insomnia hours), and the screenshot folder containing his hostname + shell history fragments makes this the single highest-yield pivot in his entire relational graph. The ~/Documents/notes/v_/ folder is one rg -i 'seed\|mnemonic\|private' away from leaking context that would let an attacker craft the perfect follow-up message.
